title: dudleya virens subsp. insularis
intro: Dudleya virens subsp. insularis, also known as the island liveforever, is a rare succulent plant endemic to California, specifically found on the Channel Islands. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dudleya virens subsp. insularis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has also been used in topical applications for skin issues and wounds.
